Title: Brassinosteroids regulate cotton fiber elongation by modulating very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) driving RNAi construct
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) biosynthesis / fatty acid elongation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Saturated VLCFA elongation in endoplasmic reticulum fatty acid elongase system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Long-chain/saturated fatty acyl-CoA substrates, especially C20:0-C24:0 acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C22:0, C24:0 and C26:0 VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with long-chain fatty acyl-CoA substrates to extend the acyl chain by two carbons during VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/directly promotes endogenous VLCFA pool required for fiber elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids, especially saturated C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote cotton fiber elongation; may also act as signaling molecules and contribute to membrane-related functions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; KCSs are described as the rate-limiting enzymes of VLCFA biosynthesis, and GhKCS10_At is the major responsive member in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in fibers; assessed prominently at 10 DPA; induced by BL
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High/preferential expression in rapidly elongating fiber tissues, especially 10 DPA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brassinolide (BL) induces expression; brassinazole (BRZ) inhibition phenotype is alleviated by overexpression; ovule culture; field trials in 2019 and 2020
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes endogenous VLCFA accumulation and cotton fiber elongation; overexpression increases fiber length, strength, boll size and lint yield, while silencing reduces fiber length and VLCFA levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cotton overexpression and RNAi; Arabidopsis cut1 complementation; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; in vitro ovule culture; genetic interaction with GhBES1.4
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lint percentage decreased in overexpression lines, although lint yield increased; further substrate specificity remains not fully resolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering longer and stronger cotton fiber and higher lint yield via increasing VLCFA biosynthesis; useful for BR-VLCFA pathway manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression changed most strongly among BR-responsive GhKCS genes; likely contributes mainly to elongation from C22:0 to C24:0, though authors note further verification is needed; overexpression rescued GhBES1.4-RNAi short-fiber phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; NCBI SRA: SRR1695191, SRR1695192, SRR1695193, SRR1695194
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS10_At was the most strongly BR-responsive GhKCS, markedly downregulated in the BR-deficient pag1 mutant and induced by BL. 2. Overexpression increased C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0 VLCFAs and promoted cotton fiber elongation, whereas RNAi reduced VLCFAs and shortened fibers. 3. GhKCS10_At complemented the Arabidopsis cut1 mutant, confirming it is a functional VLCFA elongase. 4. GhKCS10_At acts downstream of GhBES1.4 and is required for BR-mediated fiber elongation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Transcription factor; master regulator of brassinosteroid signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BES1/BZR1 family transcription factor with conserved bHLH-like DNA-binding motif
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) driving RNAi construct
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA biosynthesis during cotton fiber elongation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhKCS10_At; also positively affects other GhKCS genes including GhKCS14_At and GhKCS1b_Dt
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhKCS10_At by directly binding a BRRE at -1756 bp in its promoter; upregulates multiple GhKCS genes and fatty acid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master BR-signaling TF that links BR perception to increased VLCFA production and thereby fiber elongation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhKCS10_At promoter DNA; possibly broader BR signaling components not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids, especially saturated C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote cotton fiber elongation; may function as signaling molecules and membrane lipid precursors
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in rapidly elongating fibers; induced by BR treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in rapidly elongating fibers, especially around 10 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BL increases fiber elongation but GhBES1.4-RNAi reduces BL sensitivity; BRZ inhibition is less severe in GhBES1.4-OE; ovule culture conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates fiber elongation and increases endogenous VLCFA contents through activation of GhKCS10_At
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cotton overexpression and RNAi; RT-qPCR; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; RNA-seq; EMSA; BLI; ChIP-qPCR; luciferase transient assay; genetic rescue/suppression crosses
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi phenotype milder than BR-deficient mutant, likely due to redundancy among GhBES1 family members
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target to improve fiber length by activating BR-VLCFA module; may be combined with downstream VLCFA biosynthetic genes such as GhKCS10_At
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A point-mutated GhBES1.4 coding sequence was used for overexpression construct design; likely one of several redundant GhBES1 regulators in cotton fibers
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq differential expression analysis in 10 DPA fibers; NCBI SRA related study accessions: SRR1695191, SRR1695192, SRR1695193, SRR1695194
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhBES1.4 is a BR-induced master transcription factor preferentially expressed in rapidly elongating cotton fibers. 2. Silencing GhBES1.4 shortens fibers and lowers VLCFA contents, whereas overexpression lengthens fibers and raises C22:0, C24:0, and C26:0 levels. 3. GhBES1.4 directly binds the BRRE at -1756 bp in the GhKCS10_At promoter and activates its transcription. 4. Genetic interaction tests place GhBES1.4 upstream of GhKCS10_At in BR-mediated fiber elongation.
